clues.clue: Created in 1967 by franchisee Jim Delligatti, this double-decker sandwich has a 'special sauce.'
Introduced by Wendy's in 1969, this dairy dessert is famously too thick to drink through a straw.
McDonald's Filet-O-Fish was created in 1962 to serve Catholic customers during this meatless season.
This grilled, hexagonal Taco Bell item folds a tostada and fillings into a tortilla pocket.
Debuting nationally in 2010, this KFC sandwich uses two fried chicken filets in place of a bun.
Named for a mythic firebird, this is the most populous U.S. state capital.
This capital honoring the fourth U.S. president lies in a state named for a different leader.
Kentucky's capital is this small city, not the larger Louisville or Lexington.
Nevada's capital is named for a famed 19th-century frontiersman and scout.
South Dakota's capital, pronounced 'peer,' is spelled this distinctive way.
The 'One Saturday Morning' block debuted in 1997 on this broadcast network.
The 1987 'Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles' cartoon adapted comics by Kevin Eastman and this co-creator.
'X-Men: The Animated Series' premiered on the Fox Kids block in this year.
'Captain Planet' was conceived by this media mogul who founded CNN and TBS.
'Inspector Gadget' and 'The Real Ghostbusters' came from this French-founded studio known by a three-letter acronym.
The late-90s Taco Bell Chihuahua delivered its 'Yo quiero' catchphrase in this language.
Wendy's freckled mascot is named for the founder's daughter, whose actual first name is this.
The McDonald's Hamburglar is known for muttering this nonsense exclamation.
KFC's founder received his honorary 'Colonel' commission from the commonwealth of this state.
The 1984 'Where's the beef?' Wendy's ad starred this elderly actress, surname Peller.
Founded in 1940 in San Bernardino, this chain was transformed after Ray Kroc joined in 1955.
Founded in 1954 in Miami, this chain trademarked the 'Whopper' three years later.
Dave Thomas launched this square-patty chain in Columbus, Ohio in 1969.
Harry and Esther Snyder opened this chain, California's first drive-thru, in 1948.
Founded in 1921 in Wichita, Kansas, this chain is often called America's first hamburger chain.

final.clue: On a standard six-sided die, every pair of opposite faces always adds up to this number.
